_comment: SPL 2.0 Cookbook Catalog — single source of truth for all recipes.

_schema.is_active: bool   — included in default batch run (python run_all.py)
_schema.approval_status: string — new | approved | wip | disabled | rejected
_schema.category: string — basics | agentic | reasoning | safety | retrieval | multi-agent | application | benchmarking

recipes.name: Hello World
recipes.description: Minimal SPL program — verify spl2 + adapter + model work

recipes.name: Ollama Proxy
recipes.description: General-purpose LLM query — proxy any Ollama model
args: prompt=Explain quantum computing in one sentence

recipes.name: Multilingual Greeting
recipes.description: Greet in any language — parametric context demo

recipes.name: Model Showdown
recipes.description: Same prompt to multiple models — compare output and latency via parallel CTEs
args: prompt=Write a poem about Spring season

recipes.name: Self-Refine
recipes.description: Iterative improvement: draft → critique → refine loop

recipes.name: ReAct Agent
recipes.description: Population growth via WebSearch + Python tool — requires claude_cli adapter and --allowed-tools WebSearch
recipes.exclude_reason: model refusal on population query with safety-tuned models; use ollama only
recipes.notes: requires claude_cli adapter + WebSearch tool; momagrid does not support tool-use or claude_cli forwarding. Excluded from momagrid batch runs.

recipes.name: Safe Generation
recipes.description: Exception handling for production LLM safety

recipes.name: RAG Query
recipes.description: Retrieval-augmented generation over indexed documents — requires: spl2 rag add <doc>

recipes.name: Chain of Thought
recipes.description: Multi-step reasoning: Research → Analyze → Summarize

recipes.name: Batch Test
recipes.description: Automated testing of cookbook recipes across models via parallel CTEs

recipes.name: Debate Arena
recipes.description: Adversarial debate between two LLM personas with a judge

recipes.name: Plan and Execute
recipes.description: Planner decomposes task into steps, executor runs each one
args: task=Build a REST API for a todo app

recipes.name: Map-Reduce Summarizer
recipes.description: Split large docs into chunks, summarize each, combine results
args: document=The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This is a test document for map-reduce summarization.

recipes.name: Multi-Agent Collaboration
recipes.description: Researcher → Analyst → Writer collaboration via PROCEDURE

recipes.name: Code Review
recipes.description: Multi-pass review: security, performance, style, bugs

recipes.name: Reflection Agent
recipes.description: Meta-cognitive loop: solve → reflect → correct until confident

recipes.name: Tree of Thought
recipes.description: Explore multiple reasoning paths, score and pick the best
args: problem=Should we rewrite the legacy system or incrementally refactor?

recipes.name: Guardrails Pipeline
recipes.description: Input/output safety pipeline with PII detection and filtering

recipes.name: Memory Conversation
recipes.description: Persistent memory across conversations via memory.get/set
args: user_input=My name is Alice and I am a data scientist

recipes.name: Ensemble Voting
recipes.description: Generate multiple answers, score and vote for consensus

recipes.name: Multi-Model Pipeline
recipes.description: Per-step model selection with GENERATE...USING MODEL and quality loop

recipes.name: Text2SPL Demo
recipes.description: Natural language to SPL 2.0 compiler — prompt, workflow, and auto modes

recipes.name: Structured Output
recipes.description: CREATE FUNCTION with JSON schema — extract typed data from free text
args: text=John Smith, 42, joined Acme Corp in March 2021 earning $95,000/year

recipes.name: Few-Shot Prompting
recipes.description: Gold-standard examples embedded in SELECT context — in-context learning
args: text=The quarterly results exceeded all analyst forecasts by a significant margin

recipes.name: Nested Procedures
recipes.description: PROCEDURE calling PROCEDURE — deep composability without external orchestration

recipes.name: Prompt A/B Test
recipes.description: CTEs + EVALUATE scoring — compare two prompt variants, pick winner automatically
args: prompt_b=Give a technical explanation with analogies

recipes.name: Data Extraction
recipes.description: Pull structured fields from messy text — names, dates, amounts, references
args: text=Please process payment of USD 4,250.00 to Riverside Consulting (ref: PO-8821) by end of March.

recipes.name: Customer Support Triage
recipes.description: Classify → route → draft response in one workflow
args: ticket=My account has been charged twice for the same order #12345

recipes.name: Meeting Notes to Actions
recipes.description: Transcript in, structured TODO list + owners out
args: transcript=Alice: we need to fix the login bug before Friday. Bob: I'll handle it. Alice: also need to update the docs

recipes.name: Code Generator + Tests
recipes.description: Generate a function then generate its unit tests in one workflow
args: spec=A function that validates an email address

recipes.name: Sentiment Pipeline
recipes.description: Batch sentiment over a list, aggregate trend statistics
args: items=Great product, love it! | Terrible experience, never again | It was okay I guess

recipes.name: Socratic Tutor
recipes.description: Ask guiding questions rather than giving answers — persona-constrained generation
args: topic=Why does the sky appear blue?

recipes.name: Interview Simulator
recipes.description: Two-persona structured Q&A with per-question scoring and overall evaluation

recipes.name: Progressive Summarizer
recipes.description: Layered summary: sentence → paragraph → page with fidelity verification
args: text=Artificial intelligence has transformed industries from healthcare to finance, enabling automation of complex tasks that previously required human expertise. Machine learning models can now diagnose diseases from medical images, detect fraud in financial transactions, and generate human-like text. However, these advances raise important questions about bias, accountability, and the future of work.

recipes.name: Hypothesis Tester
recipes.description: Generate hypothesis → design test → evaluate evidence → conclude
args: observation=Remote teams show lower productivity in the first month after joining

recipes.name: Tool-Use / Function-Call
recipes.description: Register Python functions as CALL-able tools — deterministic math, zero LLM cost

recipes.name: Headline News Aggregator
recipes.description: Generate → expand → evaluate coverage → daily news digest for any topic area

recipes.name: Bedrock Quickstart
recipes.description: Fan out to three AWS Bedrock model families (Claude + Nova) and compare — adapter smoke test for enterprise environments
args: prompt=Explain the CAP theorem in two sentences.

recipes.name: Vertex AI Quickstart
recipes.description: Fan out to three Gemini tiers (Pro / Flash / Lite) on GCP Vertex AI and compare — adapter smoke test for GCP environments
args: prompt=Explain the CAP theorem in two sentences.

recipes.name: Azure OpenAI Quickstart
recipes.description: Fan out to three Azure OpenAI deployments (GPT-4o / GPT-4o mini / GPT-3.5 Turbo) and compare — adapter smoke test for Azure environments
args: prompt=Explain the CAP theorem in two sentences.

recipes.name: Human Steering
recipes.description: Human-in-the-loop: draft → pause for stdin feedback → conditionally refine — demonstrates EVALUATE on human input
recipes.exclude_reason: requires interactive stdin; incompatible with batch/nohup execution
recipes.notes: requires interactive stdin (human feedback loop); incompatible with nohup/batch execution. Run interactively only.

recipes.name: Knowledge Synthesis
recipes.description: RAG-writer pattern: extract 3 insights via LLM, persist to vector store via CALL rag_update — agents that write back to the knowledge base
args: raw_text=Recent advances in sparse attention mechanisms dramatically reduce transformer memory footprint while preserving model quality on long-context tasks.

recipes.name: Prompt Self-Tuning
recipes.description: Meta-programming: generate two improved prompt variants, A/B test via check_quality, select winner — automated prompt optimization
args: failed_case=The document describes a complex quantum algorithm.

recipes.name: Adaptive Failover
recipes.description: Resilience: try primary model → quality gate → auto-failover to stronger model if needed — GENERATE USING MODEL @var
args: query=Explain quantum entanglement for a technical audience.

recipes.name: Vision to Action
recipes.description: LLM classifies image description into DELIVERY/INTRUSION/IGNORE — deterministic EVALUATE decision tree, zero-cost routing
args: image_description=Image shows a package being delivered to the front door.

recipes.name: MCP Integration
recipes.description: Discover and invoke tools via Model Context Protocol; LLM selects tool and parameters from the discovered tool list; dispatches via HTTP to an MCP server or falls back to a local embedded implementation — DODA across deployment modes via a single EVALUATE branch

recipes.name: arXiv Morning Brief
recipes.description: Download arXiv PDFs → chunk → summarize each → reduce → assemble Markdown brief — WHILE loop, list tools, WITH OUTPUT BUDGET

recipes.name: Credit Risk Assessment
recipes.description: Fin-service: deterministic score gate → LLM qualitative review → EVALUATE routing — APPROVED / MANUAL_REVIEW / REJECTED
args: applicant_data=Applicant: Jane Doe | Income: $72,000 | Debt: $18,000 | Employment: 5 years | Prior defaults: none

recipes.name: Regulatory News Audit
recipes.description: Fin-service: WHILE loop over news feed — LLM compliance audit per item, JSON risk extraction, CRITICAL ALERT on high-risk findings

recipes.name: Code Pipeline
recipes.description: NDD closure: generate → review → improve → test → document → spec_judge (TEXT → TEXT)
args: spec=Write a binary search function that returns the index or -1

recipes.name: Image Caption
recipes.description: Describe / analyse / OCR an image using Gemma4 vision (IMAGE → TEXT)

recipes.name: Audio Summary
recipes.description: Transcribe / summarise / extract key points from audio (AUDIO → TEXT)
recipes.disabled_reason: Upstream limitation: LFM-2.5 rejects audio input on both OpenRouter (404 'No endpoints found that support input audio') and Ollama (400 Bad Request). Blocked until LFM-2.5 audio-input support lands upstream.

recipes.name: Video Summary
recipes.description: Summarise / transcribe / extract key moments from a video using gemma4 (VIDEO → TEXT)

recipes.name: Text to Image
recipes.description: Generate an image from a text prompt using gpt-image-1 (TEXT → IMAGE)
args: A serene mountain lake at golden hour, photorealistic

recipes.name: Text to Speech
recipes.description: Convert text to natural speech and save as audio file (TEXT → AUDIO)

recipes.name: Text to Video
recipes.description: Generate a video clip from a text prompt via Google Veo 2 / RunwayML (TEXT → VIDEO)
args: A duck walking through a quiet forest at dawn

recipes.name: Image Format Conversion
recipes.description: Convert image between formats: PNG ↔ JPEG ↔ WebP ↔ BMP (IMAGE → IMAGE, codec only)

recipes.name: Image Restyle
recipes.description: Analyse image with Gemma4 vision, restyle with DALL-E 3 (IMAGE+TEXT → TEXT+IMAGE)
args: watercolor painting, soft edges, pastel tones

recipes.name: Audio Format Conversion
recipes.description: Convert audio between formats: WAV ↔ MP3 ↔ OGG ↔ FLAC (AUDIO → AUDIO, codec only)

recipes.name: Voice Dialogue
recipes.description: Full voice assistant pipeline: transcribe → respond → speak (AUDIO+TEXT → TEXT+AUDIO)
recipes.disabled_reason: Upstream limitation: LFM-2.5 transcription step rejects audio input on both OpenRouter (404 'No endpoints found that support input audio') and Ollama (400 Bad Request) — same root cause as recipe 52. Blocked until LFM-2.5 audio-input support lands upstream. (Also: no sample audio shipped in sample/ — args point at recipe 52's sample as a stand-in.)

recipes.name: Video to Audio
recipes.description: Extract audio track from a video file: .mp4 → .mp3 / .wav / .flac (VIDEO → AUDIO, codec only)

recipes.name: Video to Image
recipes.description: Extract frame(s) from a video; optionally caption with gemma4 vision (VIDEO → IMAGE)

recipes.name: Parallel Code Review
recipes.description: Style, security, and test-generation reviews run concurrently via CALL PARALLEL; merged into one report (TEXT → TEXT)
args: code=def add(a, b): return a - b

recipes.name: Parallel News Digest
recipes.description: Summarise three news topics concurrently via CALL PARALLEL; merge into a morning briefing (TEXT → TEXT)

recipes.name: LLM-powered SPL Compiler (vibe-splc)
recipes.description: Compiles a .spl workflow to a target framework (langgraph / autogen / crewai) using LLM; complements the deterministic splc compiler — run both and cross-compare to surface gaps

recipes.name: Mixed-Regime Stock Analysis
recipes.description: Demonstrates CREATE TOOL_API: deterministic Python tools (fetch_ohlcv, compute_metrics) + LLM analysis (interpret_metrics, synthesize_report) in one self-contained .spl file

recipes.name: Symbolic Math Solver
recipes.description: Demonstrates SymPy as kernel deterministic substrate: LLM parses NL problem → SymPy solves exactly → LLM explains result. Shows LLM handling only what it is uniquely suited for.

recipes.name: Answer-First Problem Generator
recipes.description: SymPy generates exact answers first; LLM writes word problems and worked solutions around them. Hallucination on math is structurally impossible.

recipes.name: Jupyter Notebook Generator
recipes.description: Persistent IPython kernel (--kernel) keeps state live across CALL run_python steps — the kernel does exact eigendecomposition and symbolic A·v=λ·v verification (SymPy), the LLM only writes prose narrative; assembles and writes a real, runnable .ipynb

recipes.name: Linear Algebra Core Concepts (Content-Cache Experiment)
recipes.description: Two independently-run workflows share one content-addressed, write-once cache (cache_get/cache_put) — verified prose generated once by one script survives being asked for again by a different script, with zero extra LLM calls; a trimmed-down rehearsal for recipe 71's full curriculum pipeline

recipes.name: Linear Algebra Concept-Book Generator
recipes.description: Domain concept graph (37 nodes/51 edges, networkx.DiGraph) + style profiles drive a graph-curriculum-ordered, style-guided, SymPy-verified concept-book pipeline — compiled to a runnable notebook via the python/linalg splc target (DODA: logical .spl view → physical notebook artifact)

recipes.name: Verify arXiv References
recipes.description: Citation-integrity checker: scans free text for arXiv citations (no '## References' heading required; repeat citations of the same id are deduped), looks up ground-truth metadata via the public arXiv Atom API, downloads each PDF, and has the LLM write a verified-abstract digest into a summary CSV — automates the spot-check a careful reviewer does by hand
args: in_refs=Smith, J. (2025). A test paper for citation verification. arXiv:2501.12948.

recipes.name: Intro Geometry Concept-Book Generator
recipes.description: Sibling to recipe 71, retargeted at intro (plane) geometry: a 29-node/50-edge concept graph where 'distance' and 'angle' radicals meet at trigonometric_ratios, plus two new K-12 style profiles (middle_school, high_school) proving styles are additive. Both build_concept_book and answer_on_demand notebooks validated end-to-end.

recipes.name: Concept-Book Generator
recipes.description: Generic domain concept-book: ONE .spl workflow + graph_lib.py + declarative {domain}_graph.yaml drives concept-book generation for any domain (linalg, geometry, mechanics, music, Chinese characters, ...). Runtime-resolved via @domain_yaml — compiled once, runs against any domain shaped the same way.

recipes.name: SageMath Solver
recipes.description: Second rung of the verifier ladder: LLM parses NL problem → Sage kernel computes exact answer (Galois group, rational point, elliptic curve rank, factorization) → LLM explains. Requires --kernel-name sagemath.

recipes.name: Lean Proof Verifier
recipes.description: Top rung of the verifier ladder (milestone B-3): LLM formalizes a prose claim → Lean 4 elaborates the statement → LLM writes tactics → Lean kernel-checks the proof. Repair loops fed Lean's actual diagnostics (≤ max_tries). Badges: machine_proved (kernel-checked) / statement_checked / unverified. Citation path (exact?) runs before proof-writing — a library hit costs zero tokens. Built on spl3/lean_bridge.py (15/15 tests green).

recipes.name: Constraint Optimization
recipes.description: Demonstrates SPL's deterministic-probabilistic boundary: LLM reads a natural-language LP problem and generates PuLP solver code (probabilistic), PuLP/CBC finds the optimal solution with a proof of optimality (deterministic), ASSERT gates on solver status (formal verification), LLM interprets the result (probabilistic). Repair loop retries up to max_tries on Error/Infeasible. Default problem: bakery production planning (known optimum: bread=3, croissants=3, profit=$60). Requires: pip install pulp.

recipes.name: Code Generation + Pytest Verification
recipes.description: Upgrades recipe 50 (code_pipeline) by replacing the LLM-simulated test step with real pytest execution. LLM generates code AND tests independently from the spec; CALL run_pytest() executes both in a subprocess; ASSERT all_tests_passed() gates on the deterministic verdict. The repair loop feeds actual pytest tracebacks back to the LLM. Demonstrates that SPL's ASSERT is a formal correctness certificate, not a proxy for execution success. TMLR appendix: Domain #3 — Software Development.

recipes.name: OpenCV Scene Triage
recipes.description: OpenCV measures pixels deterministically and cheaply, SPL's LLM step reasons over the resulting structured report.

recipes.name: Neurosymbolic Verifier Ladder
recipes.description: Capstone of the verifier ladder: ONE declarative workflow puts all three deterministic rungs (SymPy from recipe 67, SageMath from recipe 75, Lean 4 + mathlib from recipe 76) behind the same problem text via a single --param backend= flip. LLM decomposes/formalizes/narrates (probabilistic); the chosen kernel computes exact instances (SymPy/Sage) or kernel-checks statements against mathlib (Lean), returning a badge (machine_proved/statement_checked/unfaithful/unverified). enable_solver=false runs the unaided-LLM baseline for direct A/B comparison. Backed by a cross-model experiment harness (run_experiment.py) with round-trip verification across ~1,200 cells.

recipes.name: Graph Reasoning
recipes.description: Extends the verifier ladder into discrete/combinatorial math: LLM reads a natural-language graph problem and writes networkx code (probabilistic), networkx computes the exact shortest-path/bipartite/cycle answer (deterministic), ASSERT gates on tool success, LLM interprets the result and restates a Final answer line, a deterministic classify_roundtrip() check confirms the LLM's stated answer matches the networkx ground truth. Repair loop retries up to max_tries on Error. Default problem: 6-warehouse shortest-path network (known optimum: A-C-B-D-E-F, distance=13). Requires: networkx (already installed).

recipes.name: Logic Puzzle Solver
recipes.description: Adds a CSP verifier class distinct from 78's LP/numeric optimization: LLM reads a natural-language zebra-style logic puzzle and writes python-constraint code (probabilistic), the CSP solver enumerates all solutions (deterministic), ASSERT gates on a Unique solution being found, LLM interprets the result and restates a Final answer line, a deterministic classify_roundtrip() check confirms the LLM's stated answer matches the solver's unique solution. Repair loop retries up to max_tries on Error/Ambiguous/NoSolution. Default problem: 3-house zebra-lite puzzle (known unique answer: the Dane drinks Coffee). Requires: pip install python-constraint.

recipes.name: Unit / Dimensional Check
recipes.description: Demonstrates a categorical (not numeric-fuzzy) verifier for physics/engineering word problems: LLM writes pint unit-aware code (probabilistic), pint executes the calculation with units attached and raises DimensionalityError on any unit mismatch (deterministic), ASSERT gates on dimensional consistency, LLM interprets the result and restates a Final answer line, a deterministic classify_roundtrip() check confirms the LLM's stated answer matches pint's computed magnitude (0.5% tolerance). Repair loop retries up to max_tries on Error. Default problem: kinematics (car accelerating from rest; known answer: 108 km/h). Requires: pip install pint.

recipes.name: SQL Verifier
recipes.description: Text-to-SQL with a row-level verifier: LLM writes a SQL SELECT from a natural-language question (probabilistic), stdlib sqlite3 executes it against a seeded in-memory database (deterministic), ASSERT gates on clean execution, a deterministic classify_roundtrip() function row-diffs the LLM's result against an independently-authored ground-truth SQL query over the same seeded data, LLM interprets the verified rows. Repair loop retries up to max_tries on SQL error. Default question: average Engineering salary (known ground truth: $97,333.33). Requires: stdlib sqlite3 only, no new pip dependency.

recipes.name: Property-Based Code Verification
recipes.description: Upgrades recipe 79's example-based pytest verification one rung further: LLM generates an implementation AND independent Hypothesis properties from the same spec (probabilistic), pytest+Hypothesis execute the properties against hundreds of auto-generated inputs in a subprocess (deterministic), ASSERT gates on all properties passing (a genuine falsifying example is never repaired away, only broken test-harness code is), LLM interprets the verified result, a deterministic classify_roundtrip() check confirms the LLM's narration agrees with the actual pass/fail verdict. Default spec: merge two sorted lists. Requires: pip install hypothesis pytest.

recipes.name: Financial Calculation Verifier
recipes.description: Demonstrates exact closed-form arithmetic verification for compound-interest/loan-amortization word problems: LLM writes stdlib decimal code implementing the correct formula (probabilistic), Decimal arithmetic computes the exact figure with no floating-point drift (deterministic), ASSERT gates on clean execution, LLM interprets the result and restates a Final answer line, a deterministic classify_roundtrip() check confirms the LLM's stated dollar figure matches the Decimal-computed ground truth (1 cent / 0.5% tolerance). Repair loop retries up to max_tries on Error. Default problem: 5-year loan amortization (known answer: $193.33/month). Requires: stdlib decimal only, no new pip dependency.

recipes.name: Route Optimization
recipes.description: A second, harder optimization class beyond recipe 78's linear program: LLM writes Google OR-Tools vehicle-routing code from a natural-language logistics problem (probabilistic), OR-Tools' combinatorial routing solver searches the NP-hard space and returns a feasible route with its total distance (deterministic), ASSERT gates on a feasible route being found, LLM interprets the route and restates a Final answer line, a deterministic classify_roundtrip() check confirms the LLM's stated total distance matches the solver's computed ground truth. Repair loop retries up to max_tries on Error. Default problem: 1 depot + 5 customer TSP (known optimum: 101 miles). Requires: pip install ortools.
